contentBlocks.original:
फलाश पुष्पसङ्काशं तारकाग्रह मस्तकम् ।
रौद्रं रौद्रात्मकं घोरं तं केतुं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phalāśa puṣpasaṅkāśaṃ tārakāgraha mastakam ।
raudraṃ raudrātmakaṃ ghōraṃ taṃ kētuṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I bow to Ketu, who resembles the blossom of the palāśa tree, the head among the shadow-planet grahas, fierce, of fierce nature, terrible. (1)

contentBlocks.original:
धूम्र वर्णां ध्वजाकारं द्विभुजं वरदाङ्गदम् ।
वैढूर्याभरणं चैव वैढूर्यमकुटं फणिम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhūmra varṇāṃ dhvajākāraṃ dvibhujaṃ varadāṅgadam ।
vaiḍhūryābharaṇaṃ chaiva vaiḍhūryamakuṭaṃ phaṇim ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of smoky colour, of flag-like form, two-armed, with boon-bestowing gesture, wearing vaidūrya (cat's-eye) gem ornaments, with a vaidūrya crown, a serpent — (2)

contentBlocks.original:
अन्त्यग्रहो महाशीर्षि सूर्यारिः पुष्पवर्ग्रही ।
गृध्रानन गतं नित्यं ध्यायेत् सर्वफलास्तये ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
antyagrahō mahāśīrṣi sūryāriḥ puṣpavargrahī ।
gṛdhrānana gataṃ nityaṃ dhyāyēt sarvaphalāstayē ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The last among the planets, of great head, enemy of the Sun, seizer of flower-offerings; one should always meditate on him who rides a vulture, for the fulfilment of all fruits. (3)

contentBlocks.original:
पातुनेत्र पिङ्गलाक्षः श्रुतिमे रक्तलोचनः ।
पातुकण्ठं चमे केतुः स्कन्दौ पातुग्रहाधिपः ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pātunētra piṅgaḻākṣaḥ śrutimē raktalōchanaḥ ।
pātukaṇṭhaṃ chamē kētuḥ skandau pātugrahādhipaḥ ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May the tawny-eyed one protect my eyes; may the red-eyed one protect my ears; may Ketu protect my throat; may the lord of planets protect my shoulders. (4)

contentBlocks.original:
प्रणमामि सदादेवं ध्वजाकारं ग्रहेश्वरम् ।
चित्राम्बरधरं देवं तं केतुं प्रणमाम्यहम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
praṇamāmi sadādēvaṃ dhvajākāraṃ grahēśvaram ।
chitrāmbaradharaṃ dēvaṃ taṃ kētuṃ praṇamāmyaham ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I always bow to the divine one of flag-like form, lord of all planets; I bow to that divine Ketu wearing many-coloured, variegated robes. (5)
